steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts criticize current scholarship on a literary period. The question asks what the authors would agree on. Look for a claim about scholarship's limitations that both texts support.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It must widen its focus to include aspects of modernist fiction beyond style, a productive but overrepresented site of inquiry.": Neither text calls for abandoning style as a lens — both stay focused on stylistic patterns. ✗
  • "Without a major shift in focus, the vision that it presents of fiction written in the period will continue to be unnecessarily limited.": Text 1 shows that overrepresenting experimental fragmentation distorts the picture of the period; Text 2 argues the fragmentation-versus-coherence binary 'inhibits inquiry' into other patterns. Both point to the same underlying problem: scholarship cannot see the full picture, and nothing will change without a reorientation. ✓
  • "Instead of engaging in unproductive debates, it should work to rehabilitate the reputations of neglected modernist works.": Text 1 doesn't claim scholars misread that relationship — it claims they overrepresent one style. ✗
  • "Its primary methods for analyzing fiction written in the period are growing obsolete as computer technology advances.": Both texts push back against that narrow framing rather than endorsing it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
